subject: Mathematics
title: Pythagoras and His Brotherhood
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Pythagoras and His Brotherhood.

hook.text: Pythagoras ran a secret mathematical cult where numbers were sacred and beans were forbidden.

buildup.text: His followers believed 'all is number'—that reality itself was built from whole numbers.

discovery.text: The Pythagorean theorem linked the sides of a right triangle: a squared plus b squared equals c squared.

twist.text: When a follower proved irrational numbers exist, the brotherhood was horrified.

climax.text: Despite the cult's odd rules, their work on harmony and ratios shaped music and geometry forever.

punchline.text: A cult that feared beans gave us the most famous theorem.

quiz.question: What did the Pythagorean brotherhood believe about numbers?
options: That all of reality was built from whole numbers
